message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker (concurrent update)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1421,7 +1421,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.395","title":"[audit][link] Self-link establishment must not duplicate self-exit actions","description":"Focused audit follow-up for src/link.rs only. LinkSet::establish_with_policy() currently allows task_a == task_b and indexes the same LinkRef twice in task_index. That makes a single logical self-link appear twice in peers_of()/peers_with_policy() and causes resolve_exits() to emit duplicate self-exit actions for one abnormal termination. Scope: make self-links fail closed or otherwise canonicalize them so one logical self-link cannot duplicate peer/exit behavior, add focused regressions, and keep the write surface to src/link.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseIsland","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","updated_at":"2026-03-17T01:14:19.447183099Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T01:14:19.447153464Z","close_reason":"Patched src/link.rs so self-links are indexed once in task_index, eliminating duplicate peers_with_policy/resolve_exits behavior. Added regressions self_link_is_indexed_once_in_peer_views and resolve_exits_self_link_emits_single_action. Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-roseisland-link-test cargo test -p asupersync self_link --lib passed. cargo fmt --check is blocked by unrelated formatting drift in asupersync-tokio-compat/src/body_bridge.rs; cargo check --all-targets is blocked by unrelated tests/e2e_raptorq_pipeline.rs API drift; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings is blocked by unrelated src/process.rs if_not_else lint.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","link","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.395","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.396","title":"[audit][link] Self-link establishment must not duplicate self-exit actions","description":"Focused audit follow-up for src/link.rs only. LinkSet::establish_with_policy() currently allows task_a == task_b and indexes the same LinkRef twice in task_index. That makes a single logical self-link appear twice in peers_of()/peers_with_policy() and causes resolve_exits() to emit duplicate self-exit actions for one abnormal termination. Scope: make self-links fail closed or otherwise canonicalize them so one logical self-link cannot duplicate peer/exit behavior, add focused regressions, and keep the write surface to src/link.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ChartreuseBeaver","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:34:22.264563078Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:34:22.264535207Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current tree; stale-open after prior audit/fix landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","link","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.396","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:08:56.848727377Z","created_by":"RoseIsland","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.397","title":"[audit][reactor] Registration::deregister must preserve Drop cleanup after persistent deregister failure","description":"Fresh diff audit of src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs found that Registration::deregister(self) consumed the handle but still disarmed Drop even when both explicit deregister attempts failed or the reactor panicked. That leaves callers with no retry surface and can strand reactor bookkeeping instead of preserving one final best-effort cleanup pass. Patch should keep Drop armed on persistent explicit deregister failure, add focused regression coverage, and validate with rch-offloaded cargo commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaStone","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:16:02.736764796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T02:31:08.359563488Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T02:31:08.359534123Z","close_reason":"Completed: kept Registration Drop armed on persistent explicit deregister failure/panic, added focused regressions, validated via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","reactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.397","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:16:02.736764796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2524,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.397","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Patched src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs so Registration::deregister(self) only disarms Drop on successful explicit cleanup. Previously the consumed handle still disarmed Drop after persistent deregister error or panic, which removed the last best-effort cleanup path and could strand reactor bookkeeping.

Validation:
- git diff --check passed
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-fuchsiastone-registration cargo check -p asupersync --lib passed
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-fuchsiastone-registration cargo test -p asupersync --lib explicit_deregister_ -- --nocapture passed with 5 tests
- rch exec -- rustfmt --check src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs passed after fixing import ordering
- focused cargo clippy on the lib is currently blocked by unrelated concurrent DNS work in src/net/dns/resolver.rs:259 (clippy needless pass by value), not by this patch
- repo-wide cargo fmt --check is currently red because concurrent edits in src/net/dns/cache.rs and src/net/dns/resolver.rs are not yet rustfmt-clean

Regression coverage:
- strengthened persistent-error test to prove Drop stays armed and performs the final cleanup pass
- strengthened panic-path test to prove the post-error Drop retry occurs
- added explicit_deregister_error_still_allows_drop_cleanup_success, where the first two explicit deregister attempts fail and the final Drop cleanup attempt succeeds","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:30:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","title":"[audit][dns] Canonicalize trailing-dot FQDNs in resolver/cache","description":"Root cause:
- resolver host validation rejects standards-valid absolute hostnames when the only extra byte is the trailing root dot
- cache key normalization lowercases hosts but does not canonicalize a trailing dot, so example.com and example.com. fragment cache/negative-cache behavior

Planned fix:
- canonicalize DNS cache/resolver host keys so trailing-dot FQDNs share cache entries with the non-dotted form
- accept max-length absolute hostnames by validating the canonical hostname length instead of the raw dotted input
- add focused regressions for cache aliasing and absolute-hostname length handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseHawk","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123970759Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123946414Z","close_reason":"Completed: current HEAD contains the DNS absolute-name cache fix plus regressions; actual bug was trailing-dot cache aliasing in resolver lookup/store, while strict clippy/fmt failures remain outside this slice.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","dns"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2552,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"TurquoiseHawk","text":"Fresh-eyes DNS audit update:
- confirmed the live bug is the opposite of the bead title: `Resolver::lookup_ip()` was stripping one trailing root dot before cache lookup/store, which aliased `name.` onto `name` and could leak search-domain-sensitive cache results across the absolute/non-absolute boundary
- kept trailing-dot trimming only for hostname-length validation in `do_lookup_ip()`; cache identity now stays on the original query host
- added focused regressions in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` proving dotted queries do not reuse non-dotted positive or negative cache entries, plus a `src/net/dns/cache.rs` regression proving case-insensitive lookups still preserve dotted-vs-non-dotted key separation

Validation so far:
- `git diff --check -- src/net/dns/resolver.rs src/net/dns/cache.rs` PASS
- `ubs --only=rust src/net/dns` ran clean of new critical findings but is noisy on existing test unwrap/assert inventory and one unrelated lifecycle heuristic in resolver connect code
- `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets` reached remote `exit=0`; local tail then reported the usual artifact-retrieval vanished-file warning
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` is red outside this slice in `src/http/h1/http_client.rs`, `src/net/websocket/*`, and `tests/websocket_acceptor_trailing_bytes.rs`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` is red outside this slice in `src/stream/debounce.rs` and `src/net/websocket/handshake.rs`
- focused `rch` test lanes for the new DNS regressions are still running unusually slowly / quietly after compile on the remote worker; no DNS-specific failure has surfaced yet","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:20:33Z"},{"id":2544,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Start note:
- `bv --robot-triage` and `bv --robot-next` both timed out again in this repo, so I used the ready set plus live code inspection to keep momentum.
- Concrete bug is in DNS canonicalization, not another stale already-landed bead.

Root cause under fix:
- trailing-dot FQDNs fragment resolver/cache behavior because cache key normalization only lowercases
- resolver length validation checks the raw host string before canonicalizing away the trailing root dot","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:28Z"},{"id":2545,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Scope correction after deeper DNS semantics review:
- I am **not** canonicalizing trailing-dot and non-dotted names into one cache key; that would risk changing search-domain semantics.
- The concrete live bug is narrower and real: `Resolver::do_lookup_ip()` rejected a standards-valid max-length absolute hostname because it counted the trailing root dot before validation.
- Fix in flight keeps query semantics intact and only validates length against the hostname with one trailing root dot trimmed.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:01:58Z"},{"id":2546,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Progress update:
- landed the resolver-side fix in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs`: max-length absolute hostnames now validate after trimming one trailing root dot for length-check purposes only
- added focused regressions for the standards-valid 254-byte absolute hostname and the still-invalid overlong case

Validation status:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/net/dns/resolver.rs` passed
- `ubs src/net/dns/resolver.rs` exited 0
- `rch exec -- ... cargo test --lib absolute_hostname -- --nocapture` and `rch exec -- ... cargo check --all-targets` are currently blocked by infrastructure, not code: the scheduler repeatedly routes to worker `vmi1227854`, and `rch fleet status` reports that worker at `0MB` free on `/tmp`, producing `cc` temp-file `ENOSPC` failures before compilation finishes

Reservation narrowed:
- released `src/net/dns/cache.rs`; keeping `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` reserved while the offloaded cargo gates are blocked","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:06:58Z"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","title":"[audit][dns] Canonicalize trailing-dot FQDNs in resolver/cache","description":"Root cause:
- resolver host validation rejects standards-valid absolute hostnames when the only extra byte is the trailing root dot
- cache key normalization lowercases hosts but does not canonicalize a trailing dot, so example.com and example.com. fragment cache/negative-cache behavior

Planned fix:
- canonicalize DNS cache/resolver host keys so trailing-dot FQDNs share cache entries with the non-dotted form
- accept max-length absolute hostnames by validating the canonical hostname length instead of the raw dotted input
- add focused regressions for cache aliasing and absolute-hostname length handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseHawk","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123970759Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T23:23:39.123946414Z","close_reason":"Completed: current HEAD contains the DNS absolute-name cache fix plus regressions; actual bug was trailing-dot cache aliasing in resolver lookup/store, while strict clippy/fmt failures remain outside this slice.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","dns"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:04.121334149Z","created_by":"RainyRiver","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2544,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Start note:
- `bv --robot-triage` and `bv --robot-next` both timed out again in this repo, so I used the ready set plus live code inspection to keep momentum.
- Concrete bug is in DNS canonicalization, not another stale already-landed bead.

Root cause under fix:
- trailing-dot FQDNs fragment resolver/cache behavior because cache key normalization only lowercases
- resolver length validation checks the raw host string before canonicalizing away the trailing root dot","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:59:28Z"},{"id":2545,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Scope correction after deeper DNS semantics review:
- I am **not** canonicalizing trailing-dot and non-dotted names into one cache key; that would risk changing search-domain semantics.
- The concrete live bug is narrower and real: `Resolver::do_lookup_ip()` rejected a standards-valid max-length absolute hostname because it counted the trailing root dot before validation.
- Fix in flight keeps query semantics intact and only validates length against the hostname with one trailing root dot trimmed.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:01:58Z"},{"id":2546,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"RainyRiver","text":"Progress update:
- landed the resolver-side fix in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs`: max-length absolute hostnames now validate after trimming one trailing root dot for length-check purposes only
- added focused regressions for the standards-valid 254-byte absolute hostname and the still-invalid overlong case

Validation status:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/net/dns/resolver.rs` passed
- `ubs src/net/dns/resolver.rs` exited 0
- `rch exec -- ... cargo test --lib absolute_hostname -- --nocapture` and `rch exec -- ... cargo check --all-targets` are currently blocked by infrastructure, not code: the scheduler repeatedly routes to worker `vmi1227854`, and `rch fleet status` reports that worker at `0MB` free on `/tmp`, producing `cc` temp-file `ENOSPC` failures before compilation finishes

Reservation narrowed:
- released `src/net/dns/cache.rs`; keeping `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` reserved while the offloaded cargo gates are blocked","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:06:58Z"},{"id":2552,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.398","author":"TurquoiseHawk","text":"Fresh-eyes DNS audit update:
- confirmed the live bug is the opposite of the bead title: `Resolver::lookup_ip()` was stripping one trailing root dot before cache lookup/store, which aliased `name.` onto `name` and could leak search-domain-sensitive cache results across the absolute/non-absolute boundary
- kept trailing-dot trimming only for hostname-length validation in `do_lookup_ip()`; cache identity now stays on the original query host
- added focused regressions in `src/net/dns/resolver.rs` proving dotted queries do not reuse non-dotted positive or negative cache entries, plus a `src/net/dns/cache.rs` regression proving case-insensitive lookups still preserve dotted-vs-non-dotted key separation

Validation so far:
- `git diff --check -- src/net/dns/resolver.rs src/net/dns/cache.rs` PASS
- `ubs --only=rust src/net/dns` ran clean of new critical findings but is noisy on existing test unwrap/assert inventory and one unrelated lifecycle heuristic in resolver connect code
- `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets` reached remote `exit=0`; local tail then reported the usual artifact-retrieval vanished-file warning
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` is red outside this slice in `src/http/h1/http_client.rs`, `src/net/websocket/*`, and `tests/websocket_acceptor_trailing_bytes.rs`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` is red outside this slice in `src/stream/debounce.rs` and `src/net/websocket/handshake.rs`
- focused `rch` test lanes for the new DNS regressions are still running unusually slowly / quietly after compile on the remote worker; no DNS-specific failure has surfaced yet","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:20:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.4","title":"[audit][service] Reserve buffer capacity in poll_ready so clone races cannot revoke readiness","description":"Buffer::poll_ready currently reports Ready based only on the shared pending count, but it does not actually reserve a slot. Two clones can both observe Ready against capacity=1, after which one caller can fill the buffer and make the other caller's immediately-following call() fail with BufferError::Full. That violates the usual Service contract expectation that a successful poll_ready authorizes the next call on that handle. Fix by reserving a slot during poll_ready, consuming it in call(), releasing it on drop/close, and adding clone-race regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CloudyPond","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:50:03.548796130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T02:08:16.874795269Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T02:08:16.874685616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","buffer","service"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:50:03.548796130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.40","title":"[audit][trace] Keep TraceRecorder::take metadata rotation deterministic","description":"TraceRecorder::take() currently resets ReplayTrace metadata for the fresh recorder by overwriting recorded_at with SystemTime::now(). That reintroduces ambient wall-clock authority even when the caller supplied deterministic TraceMetadata, so repeated take()/rotate cycles produce nondeterministic trace headers. Patch src/trace/recorder.rs so recorder rotation preserves explicit metadata deterministically instead of consulting wall clock, add a focused regression around repeated take() behavior, and validate with rch-backed targeted check/clippy/test commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PinkForest","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-09T02:56:30.373627443Z","created_by":"PinkForest","updated_at":"2026-03-09T03:02:12.568558944Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T03:02:12.568533607Z","close_reason":"TraceRecorder::take no longer reintroduces ambient wall clock; deterministic rotation regression passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.40","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:56:30.373627443Z","created_by":"PinkForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.41","title":"[audit][trace] Make ReplayCheckpoint creation deterministic for identical trace positions","description":"ReplayCheckpoint::new currently stamps created_at from SystemTime::now(), so taking the same checkpoint twice against the same trace position yields different serialized checkpoint bytes and leaks ambient wall clock back into the deterministic replay toolchain. Patch src/trace/streaming.rs so checkpoint creation is deterministic for a given trace metadata + events_processed pair, add focused regression coverage for identical-position stability and monotonic differentiation across positions, and validate with rch-backed targeted check/clippy/test commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PinkForest","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-09T03:06:17.083408797Z","created_by":"PinkForest","updated_at":"2026-03-09T03:13:10.603716194Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T03:13:10.603691518Z","close_reason":"ReplayCheckpoint no longer uses ambient wall clock; identical-position checkpoint serialization is deterministic and targeted replay checkpoint tests passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","determinism","streaming","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.41","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T03:06:17.083408797Z","created_by":"PinkForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -4767,7 +4767,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.4","title":"[FABRIC][P2.4] Unit tests for capability layer","description":"Comprehensive tests for the two-layer capability architecture.

- Static layer tests: compile-time prevention of stale-token reuse, missing permit consumption, illegal protocol steps
- Dynamic layer tests: Cx capability grants/revokes, runtime checks
- Integration tests: end-to-end publish with capability, subscribe with capability, revocation mid-stream
- Security tests: attempt to bypass capability checks, stale certificate rejection, obligation violation detection
- Lab runtime tests: capability operations under deterministic scheduling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:22.382348039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.306015432Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability","fabric","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:22.382348039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.089400783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.198374756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.305946814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","title":"[FABRIC][P3] Import/Export Morphisms & Transform Algebra","description":"# Phase 3: Import/Export Morphisms & Transform Algebra

## Purpose
Model cross-domain namespace edges as typed namespace morphisms instead of plain routing rules. Each morphism carries independently checkable facets for authority, reversibility, secrecy, cost, and observability.

## Morphism Classification
Every morphism is classified as one of:
- **authoritative**: reversible, reply-authoritative, capability-bearing
- **derived-view**: may redact/summarize/reorder within declared law, but cannot originate authority
- **egress**: one-way export into weaker trust or replay domains
- **delegation**: temporary sub-language handoff with bounded time, budget, and revocation semantics

## Morphism Specification
Each morphism defines:
- Source and destination subject language
- Allowed transform algebra (wildcard capture, deterministic hash-based partitioning, split/slice, left/right extraction)
- Reversibility requirements
- Capability requirements
- Sharing policy for metadata and identity
- Privacy and metadata disclosure policy
- Optional attestation or private-compliance proof policy
- Response policy
- Quota and budget policy

## Five Independently Checkable Facets
1. Authority
2. Reversibility
3. Secrecy and metadata exposure
4. Cost and quota envelope
5. Observability and evidence obligations

## Transform Algebra Design
- Must stay finite, decidable, and explainable (not an embedded scripting problem)
- Deterministic core for authority-bearing edges
- Randomized or irreversible transforms ONLY on one-way non-authoritative fanout paths
- Reversible transforms required for authority-bearing edges
- NATS-inspired: wildcard capture, hash-based partitioning, split/slice, left/right extraction, strict mode

## Key Features
- Definition-time validation of reversibility where required
- Cycle detection with semantic classes (not just string collision)
- Explicit policy + enforcement that cross-domain requests produce replies only in permitted reply spaces
- Auditable reasoning about what metadata crosses a boundary
- Compiled morphism certificates as inspectable artifacts

## Relation to Existing Code
- distributed/bridge.rs has cross-node bridge concepts with sequence advance
- distributed/encoding.rs has encoding/distribution logic
- transport/router.rs has RoutingTable with EndpointId routing

## FABRIC North Star Reference
This phase is governed by the five cross-cutting FABRIC success criteria defined in `src/messaging/mod.rs` and `docs/FABRIC_GUARDRAILS.md`:
1. Keep the public mental model NATS-small.
2. Make stronger guarantees explicit named service classes, never hidden taxes on the common case.
3. Lower radical behavior into small, inspectable artifacts.
4. Keep autonomous policy loops inside declared safety envelopes with replay, evidence, and rollback.
5. Target the first product wedge at systems that need sovereignty, durable partial progress, and post-incident explanation together.

If work in this phase violates those criteria, or drifts toward the anti-goal of a grand unified fabric that makes the common case slower and less legible than NATS, it must be redesigned.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:55:08.777353354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:20.630067964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fabric","federation","morphism","transform"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:55:08.777353354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:05:02.464794729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","title":"[FABRIC][P3.1] Define morphism type system","description":"Define the typed namespace morphism system. File: src/messaging/morphism.rs

- MorphismClass enum: Authoritative (reversible, reply-authoritative, capability-bearing), DerivedView (redact/summarize/reorder within declared law, no authority origination), Egress (one-way export into weaker trust/replay domains), Delegation (temporary sub-language handoff with bounded time/budget/revocation)
- Morphism struct with: source_language (SubjectPattern), dest_language (SubjectPattern), class (MorphismClass), transform (SubjectTransform), reversibility (ReversibilityRequirement), capability_requirements (Vec<FabricCapability>), sharing_policy (SharingPolicy), privacy_policy (PrivacyPolicy), response_policy (ResponsePolicy), quota_policy (QuotaPolicy)
- Five independently checkable facets: authority, reversibility, secrecy/metadata exposure, cost/quota envelope, observability/evidence obligations
- MorphismCertificate: compiled and verified morphism artifact
- Tests: morphism construction, class validation, facet independence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkSparrow","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:32.359246780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T01:41:45.316166550Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T01:41:45.137341599Z","close_reason":"Implemented typed morphism system and validated with check+tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fabric","morphism","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:32.359246780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2526,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NATS implementation reference: server/accounts.go shows the import/export graph with stream exports, service exports, imports, response tracking, service latency reporting, and cycle detection. server/subject_transform.go shows the transform vocabulary. Key difference from NATS: our morphisms carry five independently checkable facets (authority, reversibility, secrecy, cost, observability) and are classified into four classes (authoritative, derived-view, egress, delegation). NATS has no concept of reversibility requirements or privacy disclosure policy on cross-boundary edges. The transform algebra must stay finite and decidable — NATS achieved this by constraining transforms to specific operations (wildcard capture, hash partition, split/slice, left/right extract). We keep that constraint but add a strict reversibility check for authority-bearing edges.","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:48:57Z"},{"id":2553,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","author":"PinkSparrow","text":"Implemented typed FABRIC morphism surface in src/messaging/morphism.rs with class taxonomy, capability/reversibility/sharing/response/quota policy types, facet extraction, deterministic MorphismCertificate compilation, and unit coverage for class validation, facet independence, and duplicate capability rejection. Validation: rch exec cargo check --all-targets --features messaging-fabric exited 0; rch exec cargo test -p asupersync --features messaging-fabric morphism --lib passed (19 tests). Known repo-wide drift remains outside this bead: cargo fmt --check reports formatting differences in unrelated files, and cargo clippy --all-targets --features messaging-fabric -D warnings still fails on unrelated pre-existing lints in lab/stream/messaging subject+ir files. MCP Agent Mail endpoint was unavailable during wrap-up, so I could not post the normal completion thread or release reservations there.","created_at":"2026-03-19T01:40:45Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.2","title":"[FABRIC][P3.2] Build transform algebra","description":"Implement the subject transform algebra inspired by NATS server/subject_transform.go. File: src/messaging/morphism.rs (transform portion)

- SubjectTransform enum with operations: WildcardCapture (capture tokens from pattern match), DeterministicHash (hash-based partitioning — deterministic, no randomness), SplitSlice (split token and take slice), LeftExtract / RightExtract (prefix/suffix extraction), Identity (no-op), Compose (chain transforms)
- Transform must stay finite, decidable, and explainable
- Deterministic core for authority-bearing edges
- Randomized/irreversible transforms ONLY on one-way non-authoritative fanout paths (enforced by type system)
- ReversibilityRequirement: Required (compile-time check that transform is invertible), Preferred (warn if not invertible), NotRequired
- Reversibility checker: given a transform, can we compute the inverse?
- Tests: all transform operations, composition, reversibility checking, determinism verification","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:42.503028640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.632468129Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fabric","morphism","transform"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:42.503028640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.632401495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.3","title":"[FABRIC][P3.3] Compiled export/import plans with cycle detection","description":"Build the morphism compiler that produces verified export/import plans. File: src/messaging/morphism.rs (compiler portion)

- Compile morphism declarations into MorphismCertificate artifacts
- Validate all five facets at compile time
- Cycle detection with SEMANTIC classes (not just string collision): detect when morphism chains create loops that could violate authority, reversibility, or capability constraints
- Explicitly checkable policy + enforcement that cross-domain requests produce replies only in permitted reply spaces
- Auditable reasoning about what metadata crosses a boundary
- ExportPlan and ImportPlan: compiled routing programs with attached capabilities and transform certificates
- Tests: valid export/import compilation, cycle detection with various morphism classes, reply-space enforcement, metadata boundary checking","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyPuma","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:50.539175006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:08:21.458109586Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","cycles","fabric","morphism"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:50.539175006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.738506508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.846828449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.4","title":"[FABRIC][P3.4] Unit tests for morphism system","description":"Comprehensive tests for the morphism and transform system.

- Transform algebra correctness: every operation, composition, edge cases
- Reversibility: verify reversible transforms can be inverted, irreversible correctly rejected for authoritative edges
- Cycle detection: simple cycles, complex multi-hop cycles, cycles involving different morphism classes
- Morphism compilation: valid and invalid configurations
- Five-facet validation: test each facet independently and in combination
- Reply-space enforcement: cross-domain request/reply with various policies
- Security: attempt to create morphisms that violate constraints","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:56.893387202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:06.164992647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fabric","morphism","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:21:56.893387202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:05.954047522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:06.063765567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:06.164921394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.5","title":"[FABRIC][P4] Certificate-Carrying Request/Reply","description":"# Phase 4: Certificate-Carrying Request/Reply

## Purpose
Elevate request/reply from a pattern to a contract by integrating with the obligation ledger. NATS request/reply is cheap and elegant — preserve that ergonomics on the default path while making stronger contracts available when opted into.

## Core Mechanism
- Each request allocates at least one service obligation
- When stronger semantics needed: separate reply-delivery or receipt obligation
- Callee must: commit service obligation with reply payload, abort with typed failure, or transfer per policy
- Forwarded requests carry service obligation through import/export morphisms
- Reply-delivery obligations resolved only when configured delivery receipt boundary crossed
- Timeouts are explicit aborted obligations with traceable cause, not "absence of message"

## Ack Semantics (from ack taxonomy)
- Accepted: packet plane custody for forwarding
- Committed: authority plane control entry committed
- Recoverable: durability class met
- Served: service obligation completed
- Received: delivery/receipt boundary crossed

## Operational Consequences
- Silent lost replies become protocol violations (in domains with durable tracking)
- Service export latency measured separately for service completion vs reply-delivery lifecycle
- Streamed/chunked replies become obligation families with bounded cleanup semantics

## Integration Points
- Strong fit with gen_server.rs (already has disciplined reply behavior)
- Obligation ledger (obligation/ledger.rs) tracks all service obligations
- Morphisms carry obligations across import/export boundaries
- Budget system provides cleanup bounds

## Design Notes
- Default path should be NATS-cheap (packet-plane only)
- Stronger contracts are opt-in via DeliveryClass
- No exactly-once claims; idempotency + leases at boundaries

## FABRIC North Star Reference
This phase is governed by the five cross-cutting FABRIC success criteria defined in `src/messaging/mod.rs` and `docs/FABRIC_GUARDRAILS.md`:
1. Keep the public mental model NATS-small.
2. Make stronger guarantees explicit named service classes, never hidden taxes on the common case.
3. Lower radical behavior into small, inspectable artifacts.
4. Keep autonomous policy loops inside declared safety envelopes with replay, evidence, and rollback.
5. Target the first product wedge at systems that need sovereignty, durable partial progress, and post-incident explanation together.

If work in this phase violates those criteria, or drifts toward the anti-goal of a grand unified fabric that makes the common case slower and less legible than NATS, it must be redesigned.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:55:43.170202705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:23.742410975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fabric","obligation","request-reply"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:55:43.170202705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:05:19.277077279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
